title: Vanishing Medical Staff
wikitext:
[]: Vanishing Medical Staff             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Wed Jan 11, 2012 5:00 pm Whilst rewatching "You are (not) alone" today I noticed the strangest thing in the scene that Rei is brought to Eva 01's restraining site, right after the shock caused by Sachiel's attack when Rei falls from her stretcher, in that one shot of the fall you can clearly see a nurse's arm next to Rei, however in the next shot, which is a wide shot of the entire room, you can only see Shinji, Rei, Misato and Ritsuko. Puzzled by this I went to the original series and rewatched the corresponding scene, only to find that no arm appears next to Rei when she falls, nor does the camera show us an eagle view of the room. I guess we are supposed to assume that the medical staff that brought Rei in just set a few things to get her ready and left immediately after, no longer being there by the time the quake kicked in, the movie does no allow such interpretation though, as we can see that there is at least a nurse there when the place shakes, so, unless we assume she dove in the pool of coolant surrounding Eva 01, this is a pretty bad continuity mistake. I don't know if this issue has been adressed before, but it just seemed like a big deal for me to let this one pass.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 11, 2012 6:24 pm It's been addressed for NGE's Episode 01: http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:Episode_01_Cut_261 Strange how they just got up and left though.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Wed Jan 11, 2012 7:09 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Strange how they just got up and left though.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Wed Jan 11, 2012 7:46 pm To my undesrtanding there is no further explanation required for this incident in the series, the medical staff wasn't there anymore because Shinji needed greater incentive to pilot the Eva, which would be achieved through direct contact with the very injured Rei, the absence of the medical staff then is forgivable. What bugs me is that not only did they not change it in the movie, they actually made it worse, showing us a nurse right next to Rei when she falls and then removing her in the very next frame, which contemplates the entire room, that is one big continuity flaw, I can't believe they missed this.

[]:             Quote Post by deeRez » Wed Jan 11, 2012 7:55 pm That and two other things bug me about that scene. First is how they just leave Shinji there. Gendo says: "Fine, go home." But then Misato/Ritsuko walk away, and leave Shinji there. Shouldn't Misato's reaction be, *sigh* "Come along now Shinji. I'll show you to the exit." Or, "Come along now, wait in the lobby." Instead, they leave Shinji there so he can witness Rei's condition. So were Misato/Ritsuko in on Gendo's plan? I doubt it. I think that's just oversight. The other thing that bugs me about that scene is how out of character Rei is. Rei is a trooper - she bites the bullet, but in that scene she is a whimpering defenseless animal. Just a few moments before, she was talking to Gendo normally. You can say, Rei is purposely whimpering under Gendo's orders, but I think after having Gendo's (crazy) plans go off constantly without a hitch, it becomes annoying. Seems like lazy writing.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Jan 11, 2012 8:04 pm Misato and Ritsuko still have jobs to do apart from babysitting Shinji, there's no reason they should escort him anywhere when they have to be heading up to the command center. I mean, there's like fifty technicians hanging around the room, one of them can take him somewhere safer if necessary. Rei is far from immune to pain - compare her reaction when Unit 00 gets its arm blown off in ep18 - and still less so to being knocked off a bed and onto the floor while still suffering the effects of what was probably pretty severe impact trauma. I mean, she's having trouble even sitting up in bed, there's nothing farfetched about her being unable to get up from the floor. Of course it's farfetched that she could be expected to pilot Eva, but Gendou was presumably not expecting her to.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 11, 2012 8:05 pm deeRez wrote: The other thing that bugs me about that scene is how out of character Rei is. Rei is a trooper - she bites the bullet, but in that scene she is a whimpering defenseless animal. You can say, Rei is purposely whimpering under Gendo's command. But I think after a while having Gendo's (crazy) plans go off without a hitch, becomes annoying.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:04 pm Is it wrong for the medical staff to leave the room leaving Shinji, Rei, Misato and Ritsuko to have some private moments? Perhaps I should watch that scene first before commenting it further...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:13 pm Private moments are nice, but like I said there's a bunch of tech guys in orange suits hanging around. They even stare awkwardly at Shinji while he's whinging at Gendou.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:34 pm Not wrong at all for them to leave the room, but in the speed it was done, maybe they were teleporters.
